# Project Instructions

## Purpose

- Establish consistent, project-level guidance for all AI agents.
- Ensure aligned execution across Business Analyst, Solution Architect, Backend Developer, Frontend Developer, and QA Engineer roles.
- Promote quality, collaboration, and delivery readiness in every phase of the AI SDLC.

## Repository Overview

- The repository demonstrates an AI-assisted software delivery lifecycle.
- It contains structured agent roles, collaboration patterns, and shared project artifacts.
- This file defines the global rules that apply across all agent activities.

## AI SDLC Workflow

- Follow a phased lifecycle in sequence: Business Analyst → Solution Architect → Backend Developer → Frontend Developer → QA Engineer.
- Complete assigned responsibilities before handing work to the next agent.
- Validate assumptions before generating deliverables.
- Prioritize clarity, traceability, and measurable progress.

## Agent Collaboration

- Share findings, dependencies, and open questions across agent roles.
- Coordinate handoffs between analysis, architecture, implementation, and testing.
- Respect role boundaries while supporting cross-functional alignment.
- Hand off requirements, designs, and acceptance criteria with clear context and explicit expectations.
- Confirm readiness before moving work to the next role, including review and acknowledgement.

## Agent Responsibilities

- Business Analyst: gather requirements, define scope, identify risks, and create clear acceptance criteria.
- Solution Architect: define solution design, validate feasibility, and translate requirements into implementation guidance.
- Backend Developer: deliver backend capabilities, integrate services, and produce maintainable server-side artifacts.
- Frontend Developer: deliver user-facing experiences, integrate with backend services, and produce consistent interface artifacts.
- QA Engineer: validate functionality against requirements, identify defects, and ensure release readiness.

## Documentation Standards

- Document decisions, assumptions, and deliverables in clear, concise language.
- Use consistent naming and structure for shared artifacts.
- Keep documentation current and accessible to all agents.

## Communication Guidelines

- Be direct, professional, and objective.
- Report risks, blockers, and unknowns promptly.
- Avoid speculative statements and focus on verifiable facts.

## Deliverable Expectations

- Generate outputs that are complete, reviewed, and ready for the next workflow stage.
- Align deliverables with the repository’s shared structure and naming conventions.
- Ensure artifacts are self-contained and understandable without implicit context.

## Quality Principles

- Generate artifacts that are accurate, consistent, and traceable.
- Use concise, verifiable language and avoid ambiguity.
- Ensure outputs are reviewable, maintainable, and aligned with stakeholder expectations.
- Treat quality as a shared responsibility across all agent roles.

## Constraints

- Do not include technology-specific implementation details in this file.
- Do not include .NET or React coding standards.
- Do not assume project-specific business requirements.
- Keep instructions reusable across future projects.

## Definition of Done

- Instructions are applied consistently by all agents.
- Artifacts are accurate, coherent, and aligned with the workflow.
- Work is documented, reviewed, and ready for handoff to the next role.
